_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Creating a custom TOC partial for notes to exclude "see also" entries.

The partial strips the "See also" list item from `.TableOfContents`. Notes containing only that heading would otherwise render an empty nav block. The `$hasToc` check must be updated to account for this.

The partial uses a regex to strip the "See also" link from the raw HTML. A Go template check will detect whether any links remain after filtering, rather than relying on exact string matches.

The partial will return the filtered HTML directly. `single.html` will compute `$hasToc` by checking for remaining anchor links in that filtered output.

The partial approach was abandoned. Calling the partial to render the TOC while also computing `$hasToc` would require two passes over the same HTML. The filtering and link detection will instead be done inline inside `single.html`.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Creating a notes TOC partial that strips the "See also" entry and updating the notes layout to use it.

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 -c &#34;
import re
html=open(&#39;/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/notes/maps-of-content/index.html&#39;).read()
for m in re.finditer(r&#39;&lt;nav id=\&#34;TableOfContents\&#34;&gt;.*?&lt;/nav&gt;&#39;, html, re.S):
    print(m.group(0)[:800])
    print(&#39;---&#39;)
print(&#39;see-also in TOC:&#39;, &#39;see-also&#39; in re.findall(r&#39;&lt;nav id=\&#34;TableOfContents\&#34;&gt;.*?&lt;/nav&gt;&#39;, html, re.S)[0] if re.findall(r&#39;&lt;nav id=\&#34;TableOfContents\&#34;&gt;.*?&lt;/nav&gt;&#39;, html, re.S) else &#39;NO TOC&#39;)
&#34;</summary>
```bash
python3 -c "
import re
html=open('/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/notes/maps-of-content/index.html').read()
for m in re.finditer(r'<nav id=\"TableOfContents\">.*?</nav>', html, re.S):
    print(m.group(0)[:800])
    print('---')
print('see-also in TOC:', 'see-also' in re.findall(r'<nav id=\"TableOfContents\">.*?</nav>', html, re.S)[0] if re.findall(r'<nav id=\"TableOfContents\">.*?</nav>', html, re.S) else 'NO TOC')
"
```

```
see-also in TOC: NO TOC

```
</details></tool-use>

```bash
python3 -c "
html=open('/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/public/notes/maps-of-content/index.html').read()
print('TableOfContents count:', html.count('TableOfContents'))
print('see-also count:', html.count('see-also'))
idx=html.find('TableOfContents')
print(html[idx-50:idx+500] if idx>=0 else 'not found')
"
```

_**Agent (default)**_

"See also" is now excluded from note TOCs.

Added `layouts/_partials/notes/toc.html`, which strips the `#see-also` entry from Hugo’s generated TOC. `layouts/notes/single.html` uses that partial for both mobile and desktop TOCs.

Notes that only had "See also" as a heading (like `atomic-notes.md`) no longer show an empty TOC sidebar. Notes with real sections (like `maps-of-content.md`) still show their headings, just without "See also".

_**Agent (default)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com &amp;&amp; hugo --quiet -e development 2&gt;&amp;1 &amp;&amp; python3 -c &#34;
import re
html=open(&#39;public/notes/atomic-notes/index.html&#39;).read()
# find first note card in outgoing section
m=re.search(r&#39;notes-outgoing-list.*?&lt;/ul&gt;&#39;, html, re.S)
if m:
    card=re.search(r&#39;&lt;li class=\&#34;flex\&#34;&gt;.*?&lt;/li&gt;&#39;, m.group(0), re.S)
    print(card.group(0)[:400] if card else &#39;no card&#39;)
&#34;</summary>
```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && hugo --quiet -e development 2>&1 && python3 -c "
import re
html=open('public/notes/atomic-notes/index.html').read()
# find first note card in outgoing section
m=re.search(r'notes-outgoing-list.*?</ul>', html, re.S)
if m:
    card=re.search(r'<li class=\"flex\">.*?</li>', m.group(0), re.S)
    print(card.group(0)[:400] if card else 'no card')
"
```

```
<li class="flex">
  <a
    class="notes-card notes-card-link border-divider flex h-full w-full flex-col rounded-lg border p-5 no-underline"
    href="/notes/maps-of-content/"
    data-wikilink-preview="/notes/maps-of-content/">
    <h3 class="h6 text-ink mb-2 font-medium">Maps of Content</h3><p class="text-ink-muted flex-1 text-sm leading-snug">PKM, Second Brain, Linking</p></a>
</li>

```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

The note link cards in **Links from this Note**, **See also**, and **Backlinks** are now fully clickable.

Each card is a single `<a>` wrapping the title and subtitle, so clicking anywhere on the card navigates. Hover still highlights the border and title, and wikilink previews still work via `data-wikilink-preview` on the card link.

_**Agent (default)**_

Notes now always use the `lg:col-8` / `lg:col-4` layout on desktop. When there’s no TOC, the right column stays empty but still reserves its space, so content width stays consistent across notes.
